commit.message:
feat: startup env validation (#4) and scheduler disable flag (#1)

#4 - Secrets & env hygiene:
- Add Settings.validate_startup() — checks all integration credentials at boot
  and returns explicit warning strings for: Square (fatal for ingest), Twilio
  (SMS disabled), Anthropic (chat/intelligence disabled), Deputy, Xero,
  JWT_SECRET persistence, AUTOPILOT_TOKEN_ENC_KEY
- Call validate_startup() in lifespan() and log each warning with CONFIG: prefix
  so operators see misconfiguration at startup rather than at 5pm pipeline time

#1 - Scheduling source of truth:
- Add AUTOPILOT_DISABLE_SCHEDULER env var (default false)
- Guard in lifespan(): if set, skip all scheduler.add_job() calls and yield
  immediately — prevents double-firing when an external orchestrator drives runs
- Add AUTOPILOT_DISABLE_SCHEDULER to .env.example with explanation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
docs: document the testing strategy and trust story (#154)

Three independent test oracles (STF corpus, p4testgen, BMv2 diff testing)
cross-check each other to ensure correctness regardless of who wrote the
code. This is the architectural decision that makes AI-driven development
trustworthy — you trust the tests, not the author.

- New README section: "Can you trust AI-written code?"
- Rewritten ARCHITECTURE.md testing section (three-layer philosophy)
- New docs/TESTING_STRATEGY.md (full treatment)
- Cross-references from ROADMAP.md and README doc index
- Trimmed "Why Kotlin?" to avoid redundancy with the new trust section
- Updated stale P4Runtime note (@p4runtime_translation is now implemented)

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(sidebar): :lipstick: clean collapsed icons + hamburger menu icon

Inner content uses w-full instead of min-w so it matches container
width. At 3rem only icons fit, at 16rem full text shows. No more
cut-off text when collapsed.

Also swap PanelLeftIcon for MenuIcon (hamburger) on mobile trigger.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(create-adaptation-pr): fetch nightly-testing remote before checkout (#7)

When the nightly-testing remote doesn't already exist, setup_remotes()
adds it via `git remote add` but never fetches its refs. The script
then fails at `git checkout --track nightly-testing/bump/vX.Y.Z`
because the remote tracking branches don't exist locally.

commit.message:
feat: add HTTP API server for project execution

- POST /projects: create project with plan.md content
- POST /projects/:id/run: convert plan to PRD and start engine
- POST /projects/:id/stop: graceful stop via flag + SIGTERM
- GET /projects/:id: status with live progress from prd.json
- GET /projects/_queue: queue status (one-at-a-time execution)

Architecture:
- server.ts: Express entry point (port 7440)
- project-manager.ts: manages projects/ directory and state
- queue.ts: single-project execution queue with child process
- worker.ts: forked process that converts plan then runs engine
- converter.ts: extracted from convert.ts for programmatic use
- api/projects.ts: route handlers

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix TAM+e pronoun glossing and add context-aware gloss improvements

Key changes:
- e after TAM markers (ua/na/sa) now glosses as "you" (2nd person pronoun)
  instead of "by"/"to" — e.g., "ua e silafia" = "you have known"
- e le VERB at phrase start = future negative "shall not" — e.g.,
  "E le vaaia" = "he shall not see"
- Fix Ieova e case-sensitivity: lowercase lookup now matches "O LORD!"
- Fix a'u/au default gloss from "I/me" to "me" (subject form handled
  by "o a'u" compound)
- Add WHOLE_PHRASES: e pei o (like), a fāsia (for slaughter),
  seia afea (how long?), ua ia te oe (it is yours), e vavaeeseina
  (shall be cut off), ona o (because of), and many more
- Add vocabulary: magumagu, seia, afea, fāsia
- Update manu vaefa (beasts), vavaeeseina (cut off/separated)
- Bump cache to v98

commit.message:
Fix 164 CI test errors: Notes app PostgreSQL-on-SQLite compatibility

Notes app used PostgreSQL SearchVector/SearchQuery/SearchRank in
model save() and service functions without SQLite fallback, causing
every Note creation to fail with "no such function: to_tsvector"
in CI (which uses in-memory SQLite).

- Guard _refresh_search_vector() to skip on non-PostgreSQL backends
- Add icontains fallback search in services.py for SQLite
- Skip 20 PostgreSQL-specific tests on SQLite with @skipUnless

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
refactor(agents): split pilot.ts into modular components (Issue #697) (#843)

Split the 876-line pilot.ts into smaller, more maintainable modules:

| File | Description | Lines |
|------|-------------|-------|
| pilot/types.ts | Type definitions (PilotCallbacks, PilotConfig, MessageData) | ~70 |
| pilot/message-builder.ts | Message building logic (buildEnhancedContent, buildToolsSection) | ~200 |
| pilot/index.ts | Core Pilot class with orchestration logic | ~600 |

Changes:
- Extract MessageBuilder class for enhanced content building
- Extract type definitions to separate module
- Update all import paths in dependent files
- All 1594 tests pass

Benefits:
- Better separation of concerns
- Easier to test individual components
- Improved code maintainability
- Clearer module boundaries

Fixes #697

* fix(security): resolve 5 bugs found in security subsystem scan

  so dedup-cache suppression on subsequent calls doesn't hide the first fire

commit.message:
Sprint 5: data retention, down migrations, GDPR delete/export

5.1 Data retention cron (app/api/cron/data-retention/route.ts):
- Deletes gpu_metrics older than RETENTION_DAYS (default 90)
- Deletes audit_log older than 90 days (compliance window)
- Prunes agent_heartbeats older than HEARTBEAT_PRUNE_DAYS (default 30)
- Returns per-table deleted counts
- Added to vercel.json: schedule 0 3 * * 0 (Sunday 3am UTC)
- Also added budget-alerts cron to vercel.json (was missing)

5.2 Down migrations (016–019):
- 016_rollback.sql: DROP TABLE agent_heartbeats + policies/indexes
- 017_rollback.sql: restore pre-gpu_fraction generate_energy_manifest(),
  DROP COLUMN gpu_hours, min_attribution_confidence, gpu_fraction,
  attribution_confidence
- 018_rollback.sql: DROP TABLE budget_alerts + policies/indexes
- 019_rollback.sql: DROP TABLE audit_log + policies/indexes

5.3 GDPR delete (DELETE /api/user/account):
- Requires x-confirm-delete: DELETE_MY_ACCOUNT header
- Deletes: gpu_metrics, energy_manifests, chargeback_rates,
  budget_alerts, agent_heartbeats, users row
- Anonymizes audit_log (null user_id — preserves for compliance)
- Cancels Stripe subscription + deletes customer
- Deletes Supabase auth user last (invalidates session)
- Settings page: "Delete my account" button with confirmation
  dialog requiring user to type DELETE

5.4 Data export (GET /api/user/export):
- Returns JSON attachment with gpu_metrics (cap 100k), energy_manifests,
  chargeback_rates, budget_alerts
- Content-Disposition: attachment; filename=aluminatai-export-YYYY-MM-DD.json
- Settings page: "Export my data" button triggers browser download

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: populate Sentry DSN & Stripe key in build configs, update expo-video, fix legal doc paths

- Add EXPO_PUBLIC_SENTRY_DSN to app.json (was empty)

commit.message:
fix: force content-area full-width on LNB pages — GP sidebar CSS root cause

lnb_no_sidebar() prevents sidebar HTML from rendering but does not change
the body class (e.g. right-sidebar set in Customizer). GP's stylesheet
floats and narrows .content-area based on that class, which compresses the
entire content chain — .lnb-container margin:auto then computes to 0px
since there is no room to distribute, shifting content hard left.

Fix: float:none !important + width:100% !important on .content-area for
all LNB pages. No margin override — .lnb-container handles its own centring.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: seed and resolve production business by phone number id

Root cause: Businesses table in production was empty — no row existed
for phone_number_id=945190948688309. BusinessResolver correctly queries
the table but returns null, causing "no business for phone_number_id".

Fix: idempotent startup seed reads from environment variables:
- WHATSAPP_PHONE_NUMBER_ID (required to trigger seed)
- WHATSAPP_ACCESS_TOKEN
- WHATSAPP_ADMIN_KEY
- WHATSAPP_BUSINESS_NAME

If business already exists, updates token/key if changed. Never
duplicates. Non-fatal on error — logs and continues.

Added BusinessSeedTests: seed creates row, no duplicates, skips
when env var not set.

commit.message:
perf: reduce analysis WS broadcasts and DB commits to every 10 tracks

batch_size was 1 on 2-core CT, so commits and broadcasts fired every
track. Now commits and broadcasts every 10 tracks regardless, reducing
event loop and browser re-render pressure.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(parallel): add --gremlin-workers=auto support (#251)

## Summary

- Add `_workers_type(value: str) -> int` custom argparse type for
`--gremlin-workers`
- `--gremlin-workers=auto` resolves to `os.cpu_count() or 4` (fallback
when cpu_count returns None)
- `--gremlin-workers=N` still works for any positive integer N
- Zero and negative values now raise `ArgumentTypeError` with a clear
message
- Non-numeric strings raise `ArgumentTypeError` (same as before, but
with better error origin)

## Why

`type=int` silently accepted `0` and `-1`, which would produce
nonsensical parallel execution. The `auto` keyword lets users skip the
CPU-count lookup and get a sensible default on any machine.

## Test plan

- [x] `it_returns_cpu_count_for_auto` — mocked cpu_count=8 → returns 8
- [x] `it_falls_back_to_4_when_cpu_count_is_none` — cpu_count=None →
returns 4
- [x] `it_parses_a_numeric_string_as_int` — `"2"` → 2
- [x] `it_raises_argument_type_error_for_invalid_input` — `"invalid"` →
ArgumentTypeError
- [x] `it_raises_argument_type_error_for_zero` — `"0"` →
ArgumentTypeError *(gauntlet-found)*
- [x] `it_raises_argument_type_error_for_negative_integer` — `"-1"` →
ArgumentTypeError *(gauntlet-found)*
- [x] `uv run mypy src/pytest_gremlins` — no issues (47 source files)
- [x] `uv run pytest tests -m small -q` — 854 passed, 1 skipped
- [x] `uv run ruff check src tests` — all checks passed
- [x] Pre-push hooks (ruff, mypy strict, small tests) — all green

Closes #194

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/claude-code)

commit.message:
fix(types): replace cast(Any, node) with _XdistWorkerNode Protocol (#250)

## Summary

- Define `_XdistWorkerNode(Protocol)` with `workerinput: dict[str,
object]`
- Type `pytest_configure_node(node: _XdistWorkerNode)` — removes the
`cast('Any', node)` escape hatch
- Replace `Any, cast` imports with `Protocol` in the typing import block
- Add `tests/parallel/test_xdist_protocol.py` verifying the Protocol
contract

## Why

`cast('Any', ...)` is a type-safety escape hatch that silences mypy
without actually proving the attribute exists. A structural `Protocol`
expresses the same guarantee but mypy can actually check it — if xdist
ever renames `workerinput`, we get a type error instead of a runtime
`AttributeError`.

## Test plan

- [x] `tests/parallel/test_xdist_protocol.py` — TDD red/green cycle
verifies Protocol existence and structure
- [x] `uv run mypy src/pytest_gremlins` — no issues (47 source files)
- [x] `uv run pytest tests -m small -q` — 851 passed, 1 skipped
- [x] `uv run ruff check src tests` — all checks passed
- [x] Pre-push hooks (ruff, mypy strict, small tests) — all green

Closes #203

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/claude-code)

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: emit agent-native prelude for @delegated-only and agent()-cast contracts

Two runtime bugs where __tol_delegation_verify / __tol_agent_cast were called
but never defined because the prelude gate only checked for capabilities,
purposes, and agent-native storage slots.

Replace the gate with needsAgentNativePrelude() which also checks:
- any function carries @delegated (needs __tol_delegation_verify)
- any function body contains an agent(expr) cast (needs __tol_agent_cast)

Add bodyHasAgentCast / stmtHasAgentCast / exprHasAgentCast AST walkers
to detect agent() calls without a full type-environment pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
